t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}也不是什么正经人，很可能也在拿你寻开心。
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} isn't exactly a saint either, probably just messing with you as well.

t_ch: 不，{F#她}{M#他}不一样。我愿意相信{F#她}{M#他}。
t_en: No, {F#she}{M#he}'s different. I believe in {F#her}{M#him}.

t_ch: 薇克丝出现在世间…听起来很严重，但别担心，如果是M本人的话，她一定会获得胜利，就像她的口头禅那样——「你可以咒杀一切，但永远无法咒杀『正义』」！
t_en: Vexis has appeared in this world... It sounds serious, but don't worry. If it's M herself, she'll definitely prevail, just like her catchphrase says, "You can curse everything, but you can never curse justice"!

t_ch: 可惜了，我不能亲自赶过来，但我会给你引荐我手下最好的线人。
t_en: Too bad I can't make it in person, but I'll introduce you to my best informant.

t_ch: 你不来见见偶像？
t_en: Don't you want to meet your idol?

t_ch: 你别忙着看动画了！快来！
t_en: Stop watching that animation and get over here!

t_ch: 我不是不想来，而是不能来啊！麦卡托是我最喜欢的侦探！刑事侦查最忌讳感情用事，所以要规避这种过于密切的私人关系。
t_en: It's not that I don't want to go, I just can't! Mercator is my favorite detective. In criminal investigation, you must avoid letting emotions cloud your judgment, so I need to avoid getting too close on a personal level.

t_ch: 怠惰的侦探如此狡辩道。
t_en: The lazy detective retorted with such an excuse.

t_ch: 况且，人们不是都这么说嘛——「要和自己的偶像保持距离，否则一定会遭遇幻灭。」
t_en: Besides, isn't that what everyone says? "Don't touch your idols: a little gold always rubs off."

t_ch: 怠惰的侦探妄图用名言警句蒙混过关。
t_en: The lazy detective tries to get away with it by spouting wise quotes.

t_ch: 「旁白」，帮我把线人的定位发到{NICKNAME}的手机上。
t_en: Narrator, send the informant's location to {NICKNAME}'s phone.

t_ch: 记住，接头的暗号是：<color=#dbc291ff>「今天会下雨」</color>。
t_en: Remember, the passphrase is: <color=#dbc291ff>"It's going to rain today."</color>

t_ch: 挂断电话前，侦探深邃的目光投向窗外，那里，风雨欲来。
t_en: Before hanging up the phone, the detective cast a profound gaze out the window, where a storm was brewing.

t_ch: 看来你已经得到线索了，那么，出发吧。
t_en: Looks like you've got the lead. Well then, let's get moving.

t_ch: 街头，巷口，墙角…一瞬间，你脑海里涌现出无数谍战电影暗流涌动的画面，而现在，你也将成为他们中的一员。
t_en: Streets, alleyways, shadowed corners... In an instant, your mind floods with scenes from spy thrillers, all hidden currents and covert tension. And now, you are about to become one of them.

t_ch: 先想办法见到线人吧。
t_en: Let's find a way to meet the informant first.

t_ch: （刚才不死途说的「接头暗号」是什么来着？）
t_en: (What was that "passphrase" Ashveil mentioned earlier?)

t_ch: （敲敲墙）
t_en: (Knock on the wall)

t_ch: 轻点轻点，这座楼可是《迷局游戏》的取景处！漫画第三十二话最后一格，德米特里被薇克丝咒杀后就是从这个天台坠落的！被你敲坏了怎么办？
t_en: Easy, easy. This building is the actual filming location for The Labyrinth Game! In Chapter 32 of the comic, the last panel shows Demetri falling from this very rooftop after Vexis cursed him to death! What will we do if you damage it?

t_ch: …谁在说话？
t_en: ...Who's talking?

t_ch: …换个方法吧。
t_en: ...Let's try a different approach.

t_ch: （咳嗽一声）
t_en: (Cough lightly)

t_ch: 嗞█▄嗞…喉咙有痰▀▄▄老不好，多█▄半是肺热，噗噜█▄大夫，满愿电视台▀▄指定医疗▀▄嗞…嗞…
t_en: *bzzt*█▄*cough*...A throaty cough▀▄▄that's chronic, probably█▄heaty lungs, doctor█▄ploo, Fulwish TV's▀▄designated medical▀▄*bzzt*...*bzzt*...

t_ch: 广告结▀▄▄欢迎回来█▄现在▀▄播放《迷局游戏》动画版第四十七集……
t_en: Ad ▀▄▄Welcome back█▄Now▀▄playing The Labyrinth Game Animated Series Episode 47...

t_ch: …换个方法吧。
t_en: ...Let's try a different approach.

t_ch: 「今天会下雨」
t_en: "It's going to rain today"

t_ch: …你的暗号，我认可了。
t_en: ...I accept your passphrase.

t_ch: 需要法律这张大伞的保护吗？我是「必胜律师」暗布雷拉。相信不死途已经向你展示过，我「必胜律师」的实力。
t_en: Need the protection of the law's umbrella? I am "Rainmaker" Umbralle. I'm sure Ashveil has already shown you my strength.

t_ch: 好…好帅气的伞头先生！
t_en: W—Wow... what a handsome umbrella-head gentleman!

t_ch: 你怎么去做线人了！
t_en: How did you end up becoming an informant!?

t_ch: 哎呀，小声一点，人总要赚点外快的嘛！
t_en: Shh, keep it down! Everyone needs a little side hustle, you know!

t_ch: 怎么看起来是个危险人物？
t_en: Why does he look like trouble?

t_ch: 别这么说嘛，我好歹也算个体面的律师。
t_en: Hey now, I'm still a respectable attorney, you know.

t_ch: 我不需要保护，我需要线索。
t_en: I don't need protection, I need clues.

t_ch: 没问题，我不仅是必胜律师，还是必胜线人！
t_en: No problem, I'm not just an unbeatable attorney, but also an unbeatable informant!

t_ch: 那么，这位想来就是不死途提到的那位麦卡托小姐了。
t_en: So, you must be Miss Mercator that Ashveil mentioned.

t_ch: 身为灾异，应该有许多不便，难道是希望我帮您和「异常防御部」打「幻造种身份登记」的官司？
t_en: Being an aberration must come with many inconveniences. Perhaps you'd like me to help you file a lawsuit against the Department of Aberration Defense for "imagenae identity registration"?

t_ch: 不必。我自知是不被这世界欢迎的存在，不会在此世多做纠缠。但在离开之前，我还有需要解决的问题。
t_en: No need. I know I'm not welcome in this world, and I won't stay here for too long. But before I leave, there are matters I need to resolve.

t_ch: （出示薇克丝的画像）
t_en: (Shows Vexis's portrait)

t_ch: 她叫薇克丝，和我一样，也是漫画中诞生的「灾异」。此人嗜杀成性，非常危险，我们希望找到她。
t_en: Her name is Vexis, and just like me, she's an aberration born from a comic. She's a bloodthirsty killer, extremely dangerous, and we need to find her.

t_ch: 嗯？你不是消遣我吧，这明明就是通讯运营商「乐园电信」的二维市总负责人——「月梨」呀！
t_en: Huh? You're not messing with me, are you? This is clearly Pearuna, the head of the Duomension City branch of the telecom carrier Arcadia Telecom.

t_ch: 这不是个漫画角色吗！
t_en: Isn't that a comic character!

t_ch: 漫画角色？我不看漫画的，也可能是搞错了…但这个画像就是和月梨一模一样。
t_en: A comic character? I don't read comics, so I might be wrong... but this picture looks exactly like Pearuna.

t_ch: …嗯，确实可能是搞错了。月梨是个相当好的领导，她的下属都赞不绝口。而且她目前婚姻美满，还领养了个孩子，事业和家庭都顺风顺水，怎么也不像个「嗜杀成性」的人。
t_en: ...Hmm, I might be wrong about this. Pearuna is quite a good leader, and her subordinates can't stop praising her. Plus, she's happily married, has adopted a child, and both her career and family life are smooth sailing. She really doesn't seem like someone who would be a serial killer.

t_ch: 况且，她当上「乐园电信」的二维市总负责人都有好几年了，怎么也不可能是和麦卡托小姐一同现世的灾异。
t_en: Besides, she's been the head of Duomension City's Arcadia Telecom branch for several years now, there's no way she could be an aberration who manifested at the same time as Miss Mercator.

t_ch: …我明白了。从理性上来说，这件事应该与月梨小姐无关。但我必须亲自去确认她的身份，因为「我名侦探的直觉」正在发出警报。
t_en: ...I understand. Rationally speaking, Miss Pearuna shouldn't have anything to do with this. But I must verify her identity in person, because my detective's intuition is sounding the alarm.

t_ch: 如果她真是薇克丝，那么她现在几乎掌握了二维市所有人的身份信息，可以毫无阻碍地使用咒杀。也就是说，二相乐园…更危险了。
t_en: If she really is Vexis, then she now has access to almost everyone's personal information in Duomension City, and she can curse without any obstacles. In other words, Planarcadia... has become even more dangerous.

t_ch: 有没有她的联系方式？
t_en: Do you have her contact info?

t_ch: 这是个人隐私信息，我实在没法提供。但我知道，她常去老饕小巷里面那个名叫「爆炒堂」的餐厅…一周能去个三四次。
t_en: That's private information, I really can't share that. But I do know she often visits this restaurant called "Wok 'n' Roll" in Yum Yum Alley... She goes there three or four times a week.

t_ch: 现在正是饭点，你们不妨去「爆炒堂」碰碰运气。说不定就能遇见她。
t_en: It's dinner time now, why don't you try your luck at the "Wok 'n' Roll"? You might just run into her there.

t_ch: 我，我记得那里！那是椅子生前最爱的餐厅，出道作《爆炒王》的构思地！
t_en: I—I remember that place! That was Chair's favorite restaurant when she was alive. That's where she came up with Wok Star, her debut work!

t_ch: 看起来，她也许并非与此事毫无关系…非常感谢您，暗布雷拉先生，我们这就去「爆炒堂」，希望能在那里遇到月梨小姐。
t_en: Looks like she might not be completely uninvolved in this... Thank you very much, Mr. Umbralle. We'll head to "Wok 'n' Roll" right away. Hopefully we can find Miss Pearuna there.

t_ch: 呃，容我提醒一句…法律讲求无罪推定，要小心被「解决案件」的欲望支配，越过不该越过的边界呀。
t_en: Uh, let me remind you... The law rests on the presumption of innocence. Be careful not to let your desire to solve cases take over and push you across boundaries that shouldn't be crossed.

t_ch: 暗布雷拉先生，侦探M只会被正义驱策。
t_en: Mr. Umbralle, Detective M is driven by justice alone.

t_ch: 本月客诉超四十八起，乐子神在上，我们一个月都接不上五十单。
t_en: Over forty-eight complaints this month, by The Laughter, we can't even get fifty orders in a month.

t_ch: 可是，厨师长，明明还有天天来吃饭的……
t_en: But, Chef, we still have regulars coming in every day...

t_ch: 月梨小姐是我的老朋友，就不算进去了…你们还是要多反省自己的问题！
t_en: Miss Pearuna is my old friend. I won't count this against her... You should reflect on your own issues!

t_ch: 可是作为厨师长，你也应该反省吧？你在这家店倒闭之前把它买了下来，难道是为了再让它倒闭一次吗？
t_en: But as the head chef, shouldn't you be doing some self-reflection too? You bought this place before it closed down. Don't tell me you're planning to run it into the ground again?

t_ch: 这可是《爆炒王》诞生的地方！我当然不忍心让它倒闭了！
t_en: This is where Wok Star was born! Of course I can't bear to see it go under!

t_ch: 可现在都是新时代了，谁还要吃我们这样味道、装饰、氛围都普普通通的家常菜？现在正值幻月游戏，我们明明可以蹭蹭热度嘛！出点什么「限定料理」不好吗？
t_en: But this is a new era now. Who still wants plain home cooking with ordinary taste, ordinary decor, and ordinary atmosphere? The Phantasmoon Games is in full swing. We could be riding the wave. Would it really hurt to release some "special dishes"?

t_ch: 别胡闹了，好好想清楚自己工作的目的吧。你希望客人来到我们店，是为了品味你的厨艺，还是追寻一些转瞬即逝的社会热点？
t_en: Stop fooling around and think carefully about the purpose of your work. Do you want customers to come to our restaurant to savor your culinary skills, or just to chase after some fleeting social trends?

t_ch: 料理的力量在于情感，在于生活，我们就是要用最朴素的食物，安慰那些忙碌于生活的普通食客们啊！这些你们都忘了吗？
t_en: The power of cooking lies in emotions, in life itself. We are here to comfort ordinary diners who are busy just getting through their days, using the simplest of dishes. Have you forgotten that?

t_ch: 唉…厨师长，就算你说得再感人，也没办法改变生意不好的现状啊……
t_en: *sigh*...Head Chef, no matter how moving your words are, it won't change the fact that business is terrible...

t_ch: 好了好了，你们下去备菜吧。
t_en: Alright alright, you guys go prep the ingredients.

t_ch: 怪不得椅子斯基会画出《爆炒王》。这位厨师长可真像她笔下的主角。
t_en: No wonder Chairsky created Wok Star. This head chef is just like the protagonist from her comic.

t_ch: 咦？你们几位是……
t_en: Huh? And you all are...?

t_ch: 我们来找月梨小姐。
t_en: We're here to see Miss Pearuna.

t_ch: 月梨？哦哦哦，稍等…稍等一下。
t_en: Pearuna? Oh, oh, oh, wait... just a second.

t_ch: 月梨小姐现在就在这里吗？太好了，我们想找她了解一点情况。
t_en: Is Miss Pearuna here right now? Perfect, we'd like to ask her a few questions.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，你别急，你别急…我帮你叫一下。
t_en: Yeah yeah, don't worry, don't worry... I'll call her for you.

t_ch: <size=28>阿梨！快跑！她真的出现了！</size>
t_en: <size=28>She's really here! Run, Peary, run!</size>

t_ch: 一个人影从窗内翻出，跌跌撞撞地跑了。
t_en: A figure climbed out of the window and stumbled off.

t_ch: 她跑了！麦卡托，这家伙大概真的就是薇克丝！
t_en: She ran away! Mercator, she must really be Vexis!

t_ch: 想追上她，先…先让我的刀同意，呜呜……
t_en: If you wanna catch up to her, you'll have to first... get past my blade... *sob*

t_ch: 这个厨子交给我，你们快去追薇克丝！
t_en: Leave the cook to me, hurry and go after Vexis!

t_ch: 想追上她，先…先让我的刀同意，呜呜……
t_en: If you wanna catch up to her, you'll have to first... get past my blade... *sob*

t_ch: 这个厨子交给我，你们快去追薇克丝！
t_en: Leave the cook to me, hurry and go after Vexis!

t_ch: 算了，快坐我身上来！
t_en: Forget it, just get on me, quickly.

t_ch: （端坐）
t_en: (Sit upright)

t_ch: 你有行驶证吗？
t_en: Do you have a vehicle registration?

t_ch: 少啰嗦！
t_en: Cut it out!

t_ch: 我还没考驾照。
t_en: I haven't gotten my driver's license yet.

t_ch: 没事，我算是自动档。
t_en: It's fine. I'm basically automatic.

t_ch: 你试探着坐上了椅子小姐，如登临王座。
t_en: You gingerly sit down on Miss Chair, as if ascending a royal throne.

t_ch: 放轻松，坐稳扶好——
t_en: Relax, sit tight and hold on...

t_ch: 超级椅子形态，启动！
t_en: Super Chair Mode, ACTIVATE!

t_ch: 你动力源到底是什么啊啊啊啊啊——
t_en: What the heck is your power sourceaaaaaah—

t_ch: 你猜？
t_en: Take a guess.

t_ch: 安全带又在哪啊啊啊啊啊啊——
t_en: Where's the seatbeltaaaaaah—!

t_ch: 糟糕，前面人太多我飞不过去。
t_en: Blast, there's too many people ahead. I can't fly through.

t_ch: 我从旁边绕道，你先走。
t_en: I'll take a detour around, you go ahead.

t_ch: 为避免骚乱，你们将「月梨」带去了人少的路旁……
t_en: To avoid causing a commotion, you lead Pearuna to a quieter roadside...

t_ch: 见到我们就跑，你果然就是薇克丝吧？
t_en: Running away the moment you see us? You must be Vexis!

t_ch: 都十五年过去了…你们为什么还会出现…明明，我终于能过普通人的生活了。
t_en: It's been fifteen years... why are you showing up now... just when I finally managed to live like a normal person.

t_ch: 十五年？
t_en: Fifteen years?

t_ch: 是啊，我早在十五年前就已经诞生了。
t_en: Yeah, I was born fifteen years ago.

t_ch: 普通人？
t_en: A normal person?

t_ch: 难道我就不可以做一个普通人吗？
t_en: Can't I just be a normal person?

t_ch: 十五年前，上届幻月游戏，愿力满盈二相乐园。彼时恰逢《迷局游戏》即将完结，漫画的人气高挂榜一。我就是那时候作为「灾异」诞生的。
t_en: Fifteen years ago, during the previous Phantasmoon Games, Planarcadia was brimming with Wishpower. At that time, The Labyrinth Game was about to end, and the comic was dominating the popularity charts. That's when I was born as an aberration.

t_ch: 人们喜爱我杀尽世间不平事，但其实，我并非生性嗜血。如果可以，我早就想成为一个普通人……
t_en: People love me for cutting down the injustices in the world, but truth is, I'm not a bloodthirsty person by nature. If I could, I would have chosen to live like a normal person long ago...

t_ch: 因为真的…太累了。那个世界的罪孽厚得让人看不清任何希望，所以我真高兴来到这里，二相乐园没那么多罪恶，我也可以放心过普通人的生活。
t_en: Because I was just... so tired. The sins in that world were piled so high you couldn't see any hope through them. That's why I'm so glad I came here. Planarcadia doesn't have nearly as much evil, and I can finally live a normal life without worry.

t_ch: 但你手上血债还挺多的吧？
t_en: But you've got a lot of blood on your hands, don't you?

t_ch: 那不都是漫画里的剧情吗？
t_en: Isn't that just something from the comic?

t_ch: 你漫画里犯的罪要怎么算？
t_en: So how do we count the crimes you committed in the comic?

t_ch: 你会去逮捕推理剧中扮演凶手的演员吗？
t_en: Would you arrest an actor who plays a murderer in a detective show?

t_ch: 在现实世界，我从来没有伤害过任何人…我连一只尘灵都没伤害过。请相信我，我做过的那些「罪行」，归根结底只是虚构的故事罢了。
t_en: In the real world, I've never harmed anyone... I haven't even harmed a single wispae. Please believe me, all those "crimes" I committed were nothing but fictional stories.

t_ch: 杀人预告函又怎么解释？
t_en: And what about the murder calling cards?

t_ch: 乐子神在上！那只是为动画版预热的病毒式营销宣传罢了，不要当真啊。
t_en: By The Laughter! that's just viral marketing to hype up the animation adaptation, don't take it seriously.

t_ch: 我把话说到这个份上，你们总归能理解了吧？
t_en: I've laid it all out for you, so you should understand by now, right?

t_ch: 抱歉，我不能理解。
t_en: Sorry, I don't understand.

t_ch: …你不能理解哪一部分？
t_en: ...Which part don't you understand?

t_ch: 每一部分。我从诞生以后，就一直想方设法完成那部不再有机会被完成的漫画；麦卡托即便来到了陌生的地方，也依旧拼尽全力想抓住你。
t_en: Every single part. Since I was born, I've been trying every possible way to finish that comic that will never have a chance to be completed. Even in an unfamiliar place, Mercator still tries her hardest to catch you.

t_ch: 而有超能力的你居然说——你想放下，做个普通人？
t_en: And you, with your superpowers, are telling me that you want to give it all up and be a normal person?

t_ch: 超能力？琐碎的生活里，多得是费尽气力也得不到结果的烦恼。我的婚姻一潭死水，我领养的孩子也不争气，那我该怎么办？
t_en: Superpowers? In this mundane life, there are too many frustrations where you try your hardest but get nowhere. My marriage is dead in the water, my adopted kid's a disappointment, what am I supposed to do?

t_ch: 难道要把我次次考鸭蛋的儿子跟他没用的爸一块儿咒杀了吗？
t_en: Should I curse both my son who keeps failing his exams and his useless father to death?

t_ch: 在漫画中，我步步为营、机关算尽，却只觉得惶惶不可终日。而如今，我虽然要用尽全部的努力来完成最普通的现实生活，却感到踏实、感到宁静。
t_en: In the comic, I was always scheming and plotting my every move, yet I lived in constant fear. But now, even though I have to put in all my effort just to get through ordinary daily life, I feel steady and at peace.

t_ch: 追求平凡和追求超凡的人终究是无法相互理解的。
t_en: Those who seek the ordinary and those who seek the extraordinary will never truly understand each other.

t_ch: 你们可以继续追赶漫画中那刺激又英雄的命运，但薇克丝已经死了，请让她离开你们的心吧。
t_en: You can keep chasing after that thrilling, heroic destiny from the comic, but Vexis is dead. Please, let her go from your hearts.

t_ch: 少在这装了，薇克丝。
t_en: Cut the act, Vexis.

t_ch: 你们别被她骗了。
t_en: Don't let her fool you.

t_ch: 想要平静的普通生活？可以。
t_en: Want a peaceful, normal life? Fine.

t_ch: 漫画里的犯罪不作数？理所当然。
t_en: Crimes in the comic don't count? Sure thing.

t_ch: 然而，你在现实世界中就真的那么清白无辜吗？你难道就从未按捺不住自己杀戮的欲望，犯下过罪行吗？
t_en: But are you really that pure and innocent in the real world? Have you never given in to your murderous desires and committed crimes?

t_ch: 我当然没有！
t_en: Of course I did not!

t_ch: 那么，为什么漫画家椅子斯基「病逝」的时间，与你诞生的日期，完全一致？
t_en: Then why does the date of Chairsky's death perfectly match the day you came into existence?

t_ch: 难道她不是病逝吗？
t_en: Did she not die of an illness?

t_ch: 就是她，杀害了椅子斯基小姐！所谓的病逝，也不过就是被她咒杀后的结果。
t_en: It was her. She killed Miss Chairsky. The so-called "death by illness" was simply the result of her death curse.

t_ch: 作为灾异诞生后，你一定通过某种方法，发现椅子斯基准备创作出你被拘捕的失败结局，为了阻止她，你夺走了她的生命。
t_en: After becoming an aberration, you must have found out somehow that Chairsky intended to write an ending where you failed and were captured. To stop her, you took her life.

t_ch: …连这些都发现了？你真是…你为什么这么执著，还是不愿意放过我！
t_en: ...You even found out about all that? You really are... Why are you so obsessed, why won't you just let me go!

t_ch: …都是因为没有结局…才让你有机会对创造出你的椅子斯基下手！椅子斯基那么热爱漫画，那么热爱你们…她又那么有天赋……
t_en: ...It's all because there was no ending... that you got the chance to go after Chairsky who created you! She loved comics so much, cared about all of you so much... and she was so talented...

t_ch: …抱歉。无论对我和她而言，那都是唯一的办法。
t_en: ...Sorry. For both her and me, that was the only way.

t_ch: 我明白了，我明白了…为了弥补这个错误，我将*拼尽所有*，实现椅子斯基的遗愿。
t_en: I get it, I get it now... To make up for this mistake, I will give everything I have to fulfill Chairsky's final wish.

t_ch: 为这部未完成的漫画，绘下一个结局——
t_en: To draw an ending for this unfinished comic...

t_ch: 你感到一阵眩晕，视线清晰起来时，眼前只余一张不再能说话的普通椅子。
t_en: You feel a wave of dizziness. When your vision clears, all that remains is an ordinary chair that can no longer speak.

t_ch: 你看着椅子，椅子却不再能看着你，看来椅子小姐的确拼尽了所有。
t_en: You look at the chair, but the chair can no longer look back at you. It seems Miss Chair truly gave it her all.

t_ch: 事已至此，先坐下吧。
t_en: There's nothing more we can do for now. Let's sit down.

t_ch: 一张不再能喷气奔跑的椅子，还略微有点硌屁股，无法给你带来泉涌的思路。
t_en: A chair that can no longer jet around, and one that is even a bit uncomfortable to sit on. This is definitely not going to spark any fountains of inspiration.

t_ch: 还有转机，椅子小姐等我！
t_en: There is still a chance. Wait for me, Miss Chair!

t_ch: 世上转机常有，而真能转成的人不常有。你还是先想想怎么找到她们吧。
t_en: Life is full of opportunities, but not everyone can seize them. You should focus on finding them first.

t_ch: 不死途？他怎么突然给我打电话？
t_en: Ashveil? Why's he calling me all of a sudden?

t_ch: （接听电话）
t_en: (Answer the phone)

t_ch: 怎么样？麦卡托的推理是不是非常精彩！
t_en: How about that? Wasn't Mercator's deduction absolutely brilliant!

t_ch: 你怎么知道发生了什么？
t_en: How do you know what happened?

t_ch: 你难道在偷偷跟踪我们？
t_en: Have you been following us?

t_ch: 跟踪？哈哈，可不要小看我「不死神探」的情报网啊！
t_en: Following you? Haha, don't underestimate the intelligence network of the "Ashen Detective"!

t_ch: …话说回来，你知道吗？名侦探麦卡托最厉害的特技，就是将一切细节藏于脑内，并通过「思维回廊」进行分析。
t_en: ...By the way, did you know? Detective Mercator's greatest ability is her ability to retain every single detail inside her head and analyze them inside her "Mind Gallery."

t_ch: 为了模仿她，我经过了刻苦的练习，终于学会了这一招。是不是很厉害？
t_en: I put myself through some intense training just to be like her, and I finally mastered this skill. Pretty impressive, right?

t_ch: 现在情况很紧急…
t_en: Hey, we have a rather urgent situation here...

t_ch: 不是聊漫画剧情的时候吧！
t_en: So this isn't really the time to discuss comic plots!

t_ch: 没错，名侦探麦卡托也总是能在最紧急的时刻，依靠这招「思维回廊」找出真相，解决谜题。
t_en: You're right. Detective Mercator always manages to solve the mystery and uncover the truth at the most critical moments using her signature Mind Gallery technique.

t_ch: 现在就让我来教教你吧！以后也一定能派上用场的。
t_en: Let me teach it to you right now! I'm sure it'll come in handy later.

t_ch: 唔，麦卡托在这种时候一般会怎么说来着…哦，我想起来了：
t_en: Hmm, what would Mercator say at a time like this...? Oh, I remember now:

t_ch: 咳咳…注意，接下来就是，推理时刻——
t_en: *Ahem*... Attention! Now, it's Deduction Time!!!

t_ch: 既然椅子小姐说要完成结局，那么她们去的地方大概率与作者椅子斯基本人有关。
t_en: Since Miss Chair says she wants to complete the ending, there's a high chance the place they're going to is related to the author Chairsky herself.

t_ch: 可是椅子斯基不是死了吗？
t_en: But isn't Chairsky dead?

t_ch: 可作者不是被薇克丝杀了吗？
t_en: But wasn't the author killed by Vexis?

t_ch: 有趣的地方就在这里。按照麦卡托的推理，薇克丝面对要绘制出自己失败结局的造物主一定会非常仇恨，因此她有极其充分的杀人动机。
t_en: That's where things get real interesting. Based on Mercator's deductions, Vexis likely harbored intense hatred toward the creator who intended to give her a sad ending. This would establish a compelling motive for murder.

t_ch: 虽然很不愿意承认，但麦卡托在这里犯了一个致命的错误——她混淆了故事里的薇克丝和故事外的月梨。
t_en: As much as she won't admit it, Mercator made a grave error here. She conflated Vexis from the story with Pearuna from outside the story.

t_ch: 还记得你在「爆炒堂」听见的对话吗？
t_en: Remember that conversation you overheard at Wok 'n' Roll?

t_ch: 沮丧的店员…
t_en: A downcast-looking clerk...

t_ch: 可是，厨师长，明明还有天天来吃饭的……
t_en: But, Chef, we still have regulars coming in every day...

t_ch: 薇克丝总是来这吃饭，并且看起来和店员关系不错。
t_en: Vexis is always here, and seems to be on good terms with the staff too.

t_ch: 愤怒的店员…
t_en: An indignant-looking clerk...

t_ch: 你在这家店倒闭之前把它买了下来，难道是为了再让它倒闭一次吗？
t_en: You bought this store before it closed down. Don't tell me you're planning to run it into the ground again?

t_ch: 这可是《爆炒王》诞生的地方！我当然不忍心让它倒闭了！
t_en: This is where Wok Star was born! Of course I can't bear to see it go under!

t_ch: 厨师长为了不让「爆炒堂」倒闭，买下了这家店。而原因似乎是因为，这家店是《爆炒王》诞生的地方。
t_en: The head chef bought Wok 'n' Roll to keep it from closing down, apparently because it was where Wok Star was born.

t_ch: 厨师长…
t_en: Head chef...

t_ch: 怪不得椅子斯基会画出《爆炒王》。这位厨师长可真像她笔下的主角。
t_en: No wonder Chairsky created Wok Star. This head chef is just like the protagonist from her comic.

t_ch: <size=28>阿梨！快跑！她真的出现了！</size>
t_en: <size=28>She's really here! Run, Peary, run!</size>

t_ch: 想追上她，先…先让我的刀同意，呜呜……
t_en: If you wanna catch up to her, you'll have to first... get past my blade... *sob*

t_ch: 椅子小姐也说过，这位厨师长简直就像漫画里的原型，她与薇克丝的关系看起来也非常要好。
t_en: Miss Chair also mentioned that this head chef is just like a character straight out of the comic, and she seems to be close to Vexis too.

t_ch: 那么，一个显而易见的矛盾就出现了。
t_en: So this is where things obviously get contradictory.

t_ch: 在麦卡托的推理中，薇克丝与作者椅子斯基是不共戴天的死敌关系。
t_en: Based on Mercator's deductions, Vexis and the writer Chairsky were sworn enemies who could not coexist in the same world.

t_ch: 但在「爆炒堂」，这个与椅子斯基、尤其是与她的漫画息息相关的地方——
t_en: But at Wok 'n' Roll, a place that is deeply connected to Chairsky, and especially to her comic...

t_ch: ——更名月梨的薇克丝不仅常来吃饭，更是经营者所熟悉的朋友。
t_en: ...not only is Vexis, who now goes by the name Pearuna, a frequent guest, she is also a good friend of the owner.

t_ch: 这不像是凶手所为。
t_en: This doesn't look like what a killer would do.

t_ch: 这不像是仇人的态度。
t_en: This doesn't look like an antagonistic relationship between two enemies.

t_ch: 薇克丝，不是凶手。
t_en: Vexis is not the killer.

t_ch: 不愧是你！但事实胜于雄辩，侦探的烟斗只有添上烟丝才能冒出真相的火花。
t_en: Ha, impressive! But we need proof of the pudding here, and a detective's pipe needs tobacco to spark the flames of truth.

t_ch: 关于当年的真相，我想你需要更多的信息。
t_en: About what really happened back then, I think you still need more information.

t_ch: 这里是……
t_en: This is...

t_ch: 椅子斯基生前创作的工作室，严格来说，是通过我的记忆和推理还原的版本。
t_en: This is Chairsky's studio from when she was alive. More strictly speaking, it's a recreation of her studio based on my memory and deduction.

t_ch: 在椅子斯基的姐姐公布她病逝的消息后，我曾做过一些调查，毕竟…她可是我最爱的漫画家之一啊。
t_en: After Miss Chairsky's sister announced the news of her passing, I did some research... after all, she was one of my favorite comic artists.

t_ch: 当年的漫画就停在这里。
t_en: The comic ended right there, back then.

t_ch: 被M步步紧逼的薇克丝发出了最后的杀人预告，宣布要杀害一个真正无辜的人。
t_en: With M closing in on her, Vexis issues her final murder notice, declaring that she will kill someone truly innocent.

t_ch: 麦卡托以M的名义与治安官们设下埋伏，一旦薇克丝对她发动咒杀，就会被逮捕。
t_en: Mercator and security officers stage an ambush in M's name, ready to arrest Vexis the moment she moves to cast a death curse on her.

t_ch: 而麦卡托之所以敢这样做，就是因为她自信从未在薇克丝面前暴露过自己的本名。
t_en: Mercator decided on this move because she was certain she had never revealed her real name to Vexis.

t_ch: 这也是为什么…她会这么相信自己必将获胜吧。
t_en: That must be why... she's so convinced she'll win.

t_ch: 你翻看这份手稿，在最后结局的纸页上，你看见了大量反复删改的痕迹。<s>「麦卡托：正义是不死的…你无法…」「薇克丝：到底什么才算是正义呢…」</s>
t_en: As you flip through the manuscript, you notice that the final pages are heavily marked with revisions and corrections. <s>"Mercator: Justice never dies... You shall never..." "Vexis: What even counts as justice..."</s>

t_ch: 她很纠结最终的结局。
t_en: Huh. Looks like she was nowhere near decided on that ending.

t_ch: 没错，现场遗留了大量的废稿，以及各种反复试探不同结局的批注。椅子斯基就是这样一位精雕细琢的漫画家，既有天分又愿意努力……
t_en: That's right. The scene was littered with discarded drafts and loads and loads of margin notes exploring various endings. That was exactly the kind of artist Chairsky was: talented, diligent, and meticulous down to the last hair...

t_ch: 天才诞生却只来得及为世人留下残存的遗作，这样一想，真让人不甘心啊。
t_en: To think that a genius like her was born only to leave behind fragments of her work... it's truly frustrating.

t_ch: 这是一段新闻报道：「确认！天才漫画家『椅子斯基』离世！」
t_en: We now bring you this piece of news: "Confirmed: Prodigy Comic Artist Chairsky Passed Away!"

t_ch: 「…《迷局游戏》、《爆炒王》原作者，漫画家椅子斯基小姐，已确认于今晨因突发疾病抢救无效死亡。大量粉丝聚集街头，自发悼念椅子小姐……」
t_en: "Chairsky, creator of The Labyrinth Game and Wok Star, was confirmed dead this morning after emergency treatment for a sudden illness failed. A large crowd of fans has gathered on the streets, coming together to mourn Miss Chairsky..."

t_ch: 「…悼念现场却意外遭遇狂热粉丝寻衅滋事。据悉，双方争端的核心竟是为了《迷局游戏》的最终结局…姐姐称，此类向椅子斯基寄送威胁信件的疯狂行为曾多次发生……」
t_en: "At the memorial site, tensions flared when overzealous fans caused a disturbance. Reports indicate the dispute revolved, surprisingly, around the ending of The Labyrinth Game. Chairsky's older sister confirmed that the artist had repeatedly received threatening letters concerning this very issue..."

t_ch: 这类狂热粉丝的挑衅行为直到今天也没有停止。
t_en: Such disruptive behavior by extreme fans has continued unabated to this day.

t_ch: 你也参加过吧，粉丝团团长。
t_en: You were there too, weren't you, fan club president?

t_ch: 我…我那是对狂热粉丝实施正义的制裁！
t_en: I... I—I was just administering moral justice to those obsessive fans!

t_ch: 和狂热粉互殴，被猴子拦下…
t_en: Got into a fight with a crazy fan, but was stopped by a monkey...

t_ch: 这谁干的啊？你听错了吧，其实是老白为了根香蕉和人打架，被我给拦下来啦。
t_en: What are you talking about? You must've heard wrong. Mister N was actually about to get into a fight over a banana, but I stopped him.

t_ch: 狂热粉丝的行径人人得而抵制，椅子斯基说不准就是因为压力太大才迟迟画不出结局。
t_en: It's only right that we all stand against toxic fan behavior. Who knows, maybe Chairsky struggled to finish the ending because of all that pressure.

t_ch: 至于那位神秘的姐姐，之后再也没人能联系上她，而椅子斯基的相关信息也就此消失在了大众视野，连一场像样的葬礼也没有。
t_en: Despite repeated attempts, the mysterious sister was never heard from again. After that, Chairsky was gone from the public eye, and no proper funeral was ever held.

t_ch: 一个没有明显打斗痕迹的房间，散落着绘画用具和稿纸。
t_en: A room with no obvious signs of struggle, with art supplies and draft papers scattered everywhere.

t_ch: 椅子斯基就是在这样一个地方，创造出了至今仍被大家喜爱的《迷局游戏》。
t_en: It was in a space like this that Chairsky created The Labyrinth Game, a series still beloved by fans to this day.

t_ch: 总之，先整理一下目前的信息吧。
t_en: Anyway, let's go through what we know so far.

t_ch: 一个没有打斗痕迹的房间。
t_en: A room with no signs of struggle.

t_ch: 虽然基本可以排除他杀，但如果是病逝，屋里的痕迹又似乎太干净了一些。
t_en: While we can more or less rule out murder, if it was death by illness, there are some signs missing.

t_ch: 没有药物，没有体检报告……
t_en: I see no drugs, and there aren't any medical reports either...

t_ch: 也可能是她的姐姐曾经来打扫过吧。
t_en: Maybe her sister came by to clean up after.

t_ch: 绘下结局的压力。
t_en: The pressure of completing the ending.

t_ch: 就算是漫画天才，应对狂热的粉丝也够棘手。
t_en: Even a comic genius would have a hard time dealing with obsessive fans.

t_ch: 她又是个这么细致的绘画者，要为这部轰动一时的作品绘制完美的结局，承受的压力可想而知。
t_en: Given how meticulous she was, just imagine the kind of pressure she was under to deliver the perfect ending for this sensational hit.

t_ch: 椅子斯基的姐姐。
t_en: Chairsky's older sister.

t_ch: 我所有的线人都无法再联系上这位神秘的姐姐，不仅如此，椅子斯基的亲属一栏显示，她其实已经没有在世的家人了。
t_en: None of my sources can reach this mysterious sister anymore. What's more, according to Chairsky's family records, she has no living relatives left.

t_ch: 如果是这样的话，那作为「姐姐」出面宣布了椅子斯基的死讯，又迅速销声匿迹的人物，很有可能是——
t_en: If that's the case, then the "older sister" who announced Chairsky's death and then vanished so quickly can be...

t_ch: 当时恰巧诞生了的薇克丝。
t_en: Vexis, who was born at that time by chance.

t_ch: 所以…薇克丝可能伪装成了椅子斯基的姐姐，但是她为什么要这么做？
t_en: So... Vexis might have posed as Chairsky's sister. But why would she do something like that?

t_ch: 消失的遗体。
t_en: The missing body.

t_ch: 这也是本案最蹊跷的地方。
t_en: That's the most puzzling part of this case.

t_ch: 椅子斯基离世后，只由粉丝间自发举办了一场仓促的悼念仪式，至于椅子斯基的具体死因和葬礼的情况，后续没有任何媒体报道……
t_en: Following Chairsky's death, her fans hastily put together a memorial on their own. The exact cause of death and any funeral arrangements that were made were never reported in the media...

t_ch: 这是为什么？
t_en: And why was that?

t_ch: 一位天才漫画家，顶着巨大的压力迟迟无法创作出结局却意外病逝，只由神秘的姐姐宣告死讯，自此就再也没有了消息……
t_en: A brilliant comic artist, struggling under immense pressure to complete the ending, suddenly passes away. Her mysterious sister announces the death, and nothing has been heard since...

t_ch: 而现在，薇克丝很可能就是那位神秘的姐姐。
t_en: And we now think Vexis might just be that mysterious "sister."

t_ch: 她协助了椅子斯基。
t_en: She helped Chairsky.

t_ch: 帮助无法画出结局的椅子假死。
t_en: She helped Chair, who was unable to come up with the ending, feign her death.

t_ch: 椅子斯基，根本没有死。
t_en: So, Chairsky isn't dead after all.

t_ch: 原来如此，椅子斯基根本就没有死…的确，这样很多事就说得通了。
t_en: I see. So Chairsky isn't actually dead... That would explain quite a few things.

t_ch: 那么，如果椅子斯基没有死，现在又身在何方呢？
t_en: If Chairsky isn't dead, where is she now?

t_ch: 漫画家与她笔下的反派，不是敌手，却胜似同谋。
t_en: The comic artist and her villainous creation weren't adversaries, they were accomplices.

t_ch: 我想，你已经有了一些猜测。
t_en: I take it you already have some ideas.

t_ch: 嗯…在整个事件当中，有一个人的行动非常古怪，就像…什么都知道。
t_en: Yes... through all of this, one person stands out for their truly strange behavior. It's like... they knew everything.

t_ch: 你。
t_en: You.

t_ch: 这种时候还装傻，得让你瞧瞧我「冷硬派侦探」的推理之拳了。
t_en: Still playing dumb at a time like this? Looks like it's time for the "hard-boiled detective's" Fist of Deduction to make an appearance.

t_ch: 我。
t_en: Me.

t_ch: 求求了，你刚刚才「什么都知道」好吗！
t_en: Oh come on, you barely pieced everything together just a few moments ago!

t_ch: 厨师长。
t_en: Head chef.

t_ch: 月梨小姐是我的老朋友……
t_en: Miss Pearuna is an old friend of mine...

t_ch: <size=28>阿梨！快跑！她真的出现了！</size>
t_en: <size=28>She's really here! Run, Peary, run!</size>

t_ch: 想追上她，先…先让我的刀同意，呜呜……
t_en: If you wanna catch up to her, you'll have to first... get past my blade... *sob*

t_ch: 我们起初以为厨师长是椅子斯基创作《爆炒王》的原型，但现在看来……
t_en: At first, we thought the Head Chef was the inspiration for Chairsky's Wok Star, but now it appears that...

t_ch: 她，可能就是假死的椅子斯基本人。
t_en: She might be Chairsky herself, who faked her death.

t_ch: 厨师长……
t_en: Head chef...

t_ch: 不，应该叫你：天才漫画家椅子斯基小姐……
t_en: No, I should call you: Miss Chairsky, the prodigy comic artist...

t_ch: 我找到你了。
t_en: Found you.

t_ch: 原来「厨师长」就是「椅子斯基」…真是个令人惊讶的真相。
t_en: So, the Head Chef is really Chairsky... What a revelation.

t_ch: 其实你早就知道真相了吧？你意识到麦卡托会沿着错误的推理一路狂飙，但身为粉丝，又实在不希望当面揭露她的错误，所以才拒绝直接来帮我们。没错吧？
t_en: You knew the truth all along, didn't you? You realized Mercator would go off on a wild goose chase with her deductions, but as her fan, you couldn't bring yourself to point out her mistakes. That's why you refused to help us directly. Am I right?

t_ch: …好啦！推理时间已经结束了！接下来你该去现场和椅子斯基老师对峙了。不聊了，再见。
t_en: ...Fine! Deduction Time is over! Now it's time for you to go confront Miss Chairsky herself. That's it. Goodbye.

t_ch: 那么…椅子斯基小姐，跟我聊聊吧。
t_en: So, let's have a chat... Miss Chairsky.

t_ch: 虽然早就想过会有这一天，但这一刻真正到来的时候，还是会有一种「啊，终于还是大结局了」的完结感。
t_en: I've long known this day would come, and yet now that it's here, I can't help but feel, "Ah... the grand finale has arrived."

t_ch: 你的故事可没有完结。
t_en: Your story isn't over yet.

t_ch: 我努力过，但抱歉，我没能做到让它有一个完美的结局。那位椅子小姐…是我的「自画像」吧？某种意义上说，也是我理想中的自己。也许让她来完结这个故事，也不错。
t_en: I did my best, but I'm afraid I couldn't give the story a perfect ending. That Miss Chair... she's my "self-portrait," isn't she? In a way, she's also an idealized version of myself. Maybe it's not such a bad thing to let her bring this story to a close.

t_ch: 椅子小姐正在帮你完结。
t_en: Miss Chair is helping you wrap things up.

t_ch: 她…她是我的「自画像」吧？某种意义上说，也是我理想中的自己。她大概比我更加纯粹，对于完结漫画有着更热切的渴望，可我已经不愿努力了。
t_en: She... she's my "self-portrait," isn't she? In some ways, she's an idealized version of me: purer, with a deeper and far stronger desire to complete the comic. But I've lost the will to continue.

t_ch: 画漫画曾经是我最美好的时光，我喜欢创造她们，为她们绘制人生。
t_en: Creating comics was the most beautiful time of my life. I loved giving life to my characters through my drawings.

t_ch: 但粉丝总有着各种各样的期待，角色也有着各种各样的命运，我不得不扛下所有压力去寻找一个完美的结局，可我…我只是一个爱画点破漫画的普通人啊！
t_en: But fans have all sorts of expectations, and characters have all sorts of destinies, too. I had to carry all that pressure to find the perfect ending, but I... I'm just an ordinary person who loves drawing silly little comics!

t_ch: 所有人都夸我是天才，只有薇克丝理解我。
t_en: Everyone calls me a genius, but Vexis is the only one who truly gets me.

t_ch: 她从书中诞生，告诉我觉得累就停下，「…不必在乎别人的期待，就让那些百万的读者等着好了，因为从今天开始，那个软弱的你就死掉了。」
t_en: She stepped out of the comic and told me to stop when I felt exhausted. "Forget about everyone else's expectations," she said, "those millions of readers can wait. From now on, that weak version of you is dead."

t_ch: 十五年了，该去面对了。
t_en: But it's been <unbreak>15</unbreak> years, and it's time to face reality.

t_ch: 为什么就非要去面对呢？
t_en: Although, why should I have to face it anyway?

t_ch: 别再逃避了。
t_en: You should stop running away from the truth.

t_ch: 逃避可耻但有用啊！
t_en: Running away isn't exactly honorable, but it works!

t_ch: 带我们去找椅子小姐吧。
t_en: Take us to Miss Chair.

t_ch: 我知道你很急，但你先别急，我先给你炒俩菜？
t_en: I know you're in a hurry, but you can catch your breath here first. How about I make you something to eat?

t_ch: 就让我随心所欲地画点漫画做点饭，开开心心的不好吗？为什么非要让我再次去面对舆论，面对狂热的粉丝，面对结局的压力？
t_en: Why can't I simply draw comics and cook as I please? Why must I face public opinion, the obsessive fans, and the constant pressure to complete the ending?

t_ch: 求求你，我是个懦弱的人，真的不愿再去管这个让我痛苦的漫画了。
t_en: Please... I'm just a coward who can't face this comic that's causing me so much pain anymore.

t_ch: 可是，是你创造了她们…
t_en: But you're the one who created those characters...

t_ch: 创造了她们我就应该承受它带来的痛苦吗？
t_en: And therefore I must bear the pain that comes with creating them?

t_ch: 你应该守护她们啊！
t_en: You should be protecting them!

t_ch: 那就不管你的朋友月梨了吗？
t_en: Are you really going to do nothing about your friend Pearuna?

t_ch: 其实你根本舍不得抛弃她们。
t_en: Actually, you can't really bring yourself to abandon them.

t_ch: 你一眼就认出了椅子和麦卡托。
t_en: You actually recognized Chair and Mercator right away.

t_ch: 你一直保护着月梨的秘密。
t_en: You've been protecting Pearuna's secret all along.

t_ch: …哎…你说的没错。
t_en: ...*Sigh*... You're right.

t_ch: 我向月梨承诺过会让她拥有全新的生活，这可能是我唯一能为她守护的东西了。
t_en: I promised Pearuna I'd give her a brand-new life. This might be the only thing I can do for her now.

t_ch: 至于椅子和麦卡托…是我创造了她们，她们也曾是我最美好的印记，我确实从没有一天能真正放下。
t_en: As for Chair and Mercator... I created them, and they were once my most beautiful memories. It's true that I haven't been able to let go of them for even a single day.

t_ch: …算了，都过了十五年安生日子了……
t_en: ...Oh well. I've had <unbreak>15</unbreak> years of peace up till now after all...

t_ch: 跟我来吧，我想椅子会带她们去我曾经创作的地方，我可以带路。
t_en: Come with me. I think Chair will take them to where I used to draw. I can show you the way.

t_ch: 我稍加准备一下。
t_en: Just give me a little bit of time to get ready.

t_ch: 嗯，不必着急奔赴一场结局。
t_en: Well, there's no need to rush headlong into an ending.

t_ch: 现在出发。
t_en: Let's go now.

t_ch: 请跟我来。
t_en: Please come with me.

t_ch: 哦，记得把这把椅子捎上。
t_en: Oh, don't forget to bring this chair along.

t_ch: 杀死那个背负期待的我后，我却感到了彻底的自由。
t_en: And after killing that version of myself, the self that was weighed down by so many expectations, I actually felt true freedom.

t_ch: 准备好了吗？
t_en: Are you ready?

t_ch: 我稍加准备一下。
t_en: Just give me a little bit of time to get ready.

t_ch: 嗯，不必着急奔赴一场结局。
t_en: Well, there's no need to rush headlong into an ending.

t_ch: 现在出发。
t_en: Let's go now.

t_ch: 请跟我来。
t_en: Please come with me.

t_ch: 哦，记得把这把椅子捎上。
t_en: Oh, don't forget to bring this chair along.

t_ch: 薇克丝，我最好的搭档，还记得我们是什么时候分道扬镳的吗？
t_en: Vexis, my best of partners, remember the time we parted ways?

t_ch: 你为什么不说话？好吧…我来替你回忆一下。
t_en: Why aren't you saying anything? Alright... let me refresh your memory.

t_ch: 「拉德克」，这个名字你可能已经不记得了，但他的罪行你或许还会有些印象——他绑架了仇人的女儿，并残忍杀害了她。
t_en: You may not remember the name Radek, but you likely remember his crimes. He kidnapped his rival's daughter and murdered her in cold blood.

t_ch: 我们携手追查了整整一年，但这个人却被层层保护，姑娘的死也被认定为意外。那时候你问我，正义到底是什么，我给不出答案……
t_en: We had been on the case an entire year, but he was protected at every turn, and the girl's death was eventually ruled an accident. Back then, you asked me what justice really meant, and I had no answer for you...

t_ch: 如果你的「正义」给不了以牙还牙、以眼还眼的惩戒，那么这个总是迟到和缺席的家伙，就不会是正确答案。
t_en: If your version of "justice" can't provide tit-for-tat punishment, then this so-called "justice" that's always delayed and doomed to be absent cannot be the right answer.

t_ch: 所以你的答案，就是打着正义的名号，实施残酷的杀戮？
t_en: So your answer is to commit brutal murders in the name of justice?

t_ch: 残酷吗？比起他的罪恶，私刑制裁者的咒杀还是太痛快了。
t_en: "Brutal"? Death by vigilante justice is still way too merciful compared to what he committed.

t_ch: 事到如今，你还不承认你就是私刑制裁者吗？
t_en: Even now, you still won't admit you're the vigilante?

t_ch: 够了，当初的结局就到这里。
t_en: Enough. That's where the original ending stops.

t_ch: 不够！薇克丝，就算这个人是罪有应得，那么你接下来咒杀的那些无辜的生命呢？
t_en: That's not enough! Vexis, even if this person did deserve what they got, what about all those innocent lives you took after that?

t_ch: …算我求你了，你看看我吧，我才是无辜的那一个。
t_en: ...Please, just look at me. I'm the innocent one.

t_ch: 我只想拥有普通的生活，请放过我吧，名侦探麦卡托！
t_en: I just want a normal life, Detective Mercator, so please just leave me alone!

t_ch: 你别这样，都不符合人设了！
t_en: Hey, come on! That's not how your character should act or speak!

t_ch: 咳咳，继续…无辜？那么椅子斯基的死算什么？薇克丝，接受正义的审判吧——
t_en: *Ahem* Moving on. "Innocent," you say? Then what about the deceased Chairsky? Vexis, it's time for you to face justice!

t_ch: 请等一等，薇克丝没有杀害椅子斯基。
t_en: Hold on a second. Vexis did not kill Chairsky.

t_ch: 因为我就是你们的创造者，漫画家椅子斯基。如你们所见，我还活着。
t_en: I say that because I am your creator, the comic artist Chairsky. As you can see, I'm still alive.

t_ch: 当年是一场假死。
t_en: She feigned her death back then.

t_ch: 薇克丝确实没有杀过现实的人。
t_en: I can confirm that Vexis has never killed anyone in real life.

t_ch: …你还…活着？
t_en: ...You're still... alive?

t_ch: 你看起来很虚弱，真抱歉，为了弥补我当年没画出的结局，你一定消耗了不少愿力。
t_en: You look really worn out... I'm sorry. You must have used up so much Wishpower trying to fill in that ending I never drew.

t_ch: 不用道歉，虽然不知道你为什么选择假死，但在我心里，你真的画得特别、特别棒！
t_en: You don't need to apologize! I don't know why you chose to fake your death, but from the bottom of my heart, your art is truly, truly amazing!

t_ch: 椅子斯基老师，对不起…我真的不希望再次把你卷进这本漫画的风波里来……
t_en: Miss Chairsky, I'm sorry... I never wanted to pull you back into more drama surrounding this comic.

t_ch: 不，这一切本就是因我而起。我才该道歉的，阿梨，我逃避太久了。
t_en: No, this all started because of me. I should be the one apologizing, Peary. I've been running from this for far too long.

t_ch: 这…这算是怎么一回事？
t_en: Wh—What's going on here???

t_ch: 或许，不必再追捕薇克丝了。
t_en: Maybe we don't need to go after Vexis anymore.

t_ch: 请放过彼此吧！
t_en: Please just spare each other!

t_ch: 麦卡托，在现实世界，「薇克丝」从未犯下任何罪行，也不会犯下任何罪行。
t_en: Mercator, in the real world, Vexis has never committed any crimes, nor will she ever commit any.

t_ch: 你再也不必为了追寻她的踪迹而殚精竭虑…你自由了，可以拥抱一段全新的生活了，就像我们一样。
t_en: You no longer have to wear yourself out chasing her... You're free now, ready to embrace a brand-new life like we did.

t_ch: 新的生活…什么生活？跟她一样普通的人生吗？可是…可是我是侦探麦卡托啊！是永远向邪恶追索的名侦探啊……
t_en: A new life... what life is that? Am I supposed to live an ordinary life just like her? But... I'm Detective Mercator! The great detective who never stops hunting evil...!

t_ch: 哈，哈哈！所以，你们的意思是说，我一直以来的追索都没有意义是吗？
t_en: Hahaha! So what you're saying is, all the chasing I've been doing has been for absolutely nothing!?

t_ch: …抱歉，我现在需要一个人静一静。
t_en: ...Sorry, I need some time alone right now.

t_ch: 我理解她，就像我带着完成漫画的执念诞生一样，对麦卡托来说，抓住薇克丝就是她生命的全部。
t_en: I understand how she feels. It is just as how I was born with an obsession to finish the comic. For Mercator, hunting Vexis has been her whole life.

t_ch: 其实我原本也有些迷茫，但因为椅子斯基还活着，我好像可以找到新的期待了…可惜，麦卡托还没有。
t_en: I've been feeling a little lost myself, but now that I know Chairsky is still alive, it feels like I have something new to look forward to... Unfortunately, that isn't true for Mercator yet.

t_ch: 请替我去安慰安慰她吧，她只是…在得知一切后，有些不知所措……
t_en: Please, do me a favor and console her in my stead. She's just... a little overwhelmed after learning all these.

t_ch: 真的很抱歉，如果我当年能画出结局……
t_en: I'm really sorry. If only I was able to draw the ending back then...

t_ch: 可是我真的太累了……
t_en: But I was just so tired...

t_ch: 辛苦你查证当年的真相，我原本想一直瞒下去。
t_en: I know it couldn't have been easy for you, trying to get to the truth back then. I originally intended for it to stay buried forever.

t_ch: 麦卡托…哎，她为什么总是这么执着？
t_en: Mercator... *Sigh* Why does she always have to be so persistent?

t_ch: 就像我会愿意用尽全部愿力将你们带来这里一样……
t_en: Just as how I would willingly use up all my Wishpower to bring you here...

t_ch: 麦卡托应该也会希望，无论如何，至少能在最终的结局里奋力战斗一次吧。
t_en: Mercator probably wants to give it her all in one final, concluding battle, no matter the cost.

t_ch: 感谢你带来的真相。我穷尽一生追寻正义，却被一个非关正义的真相所击垮…你会觉得我很荒唐吗？
t_en: Thank you for bringing me the truth. I've spent my whole life pursuing justice, only to be crushed by a truth that's far from just... Don't you think I'm absurd?

t_ch: 生命的真相总是最沉重的。
t_en: Truths about life are always the hardest to bear.

t_ch: 是啊，也许反而是那些关乎正义的、非黑即白的真相，更容易令人接受一些。
t_en: Yeah. Those clear-cut, black-and-white truths about justice are probably easier for people to accept.

t_ch: 你不能试着接受普通的生活吗？
t_en: Can't you at least try to live a normal life?

t_ch: 可我所追求的曾经种种，就是我所热爱且愿意付出一切的啊！放弃，哪有那么简单？
t_en: But giving up isn't as easy as it sounds! Everything I've been pursuing is what I love, and what I'd give anything for!

t_ch: 从我在漫画里诞生的那个瞬间开始，我就一直在无尽地追缉凶犯，惩治罪恶，为世界带来正义的审判。
t_en: From the very moment I was born in the comic, I've been chasing criminals, punishing evil, and bringing justice to the world nonstop.

t_ch: 就像我所说的那样，我相信「你可以咒杀一切，但永远无法咒杀『正义』！」，我也相信「麦卡托的烟斗里，装不下失败。」
t_en: Like I said before, "you can curse everything, but you can never curse justice"! I also believe that "failure has no place in Detective Mercator's pipe."

t_ch: 我就是为了这些诞生的，我就是被这些话支撑到今天的，你明白当我意识到自己只是一个漫画角色的时候，我有多绝望吗？
t_en: This is what I was born for, and these are the words that have kept me going all this time. Do you know how much despair I felt when I realized I was nothing but a comic character?

t_ch: 但我坚强起来了。因为我以为，这个世界也需要我，需要我抓住薇克丝带来拯救。
t_en: But I still pulled myself together, because I believed that this world needed me, that it really needed me to save it by arresting Vexis.

t_ch: 可现在，一切又没有了…我的梦想，我的意义，我追求的正义…全都没有了。
t_en: But now, everything is gone... My dreams, my purpose, the justice I fought for... They're all gone.

t_ch: 人生本来没有意义。
t_en: Life never had a meaning on its own.

t_ch: 真是痛苦的认知……
t_en: What a painful realization...

t_ch: 去追求新的意义与价值吧。
t_en: So go seek a new meaning and values.

t_ch: 这条无尽追求的路太痛苦了……
t_en: An endless pursuit like this is just too agonizing to bear...

t_ch: 谢谢你的安慰，现在我有点想明白了——
t_en: Thanks for talking to me. I'm starting to see things a little more clearly now...

t_ch: 我所求索的一切，都不过是个虚构的故事而已…可那又怎么样？我都已经拼尽全力到了此刻，那么就算只是个故事，我也要向命运讨要一个结局。
t_en: Everything I've been chasing... it's all just a made-up story. But so what? I've given it my all to get to this point, so even if it's just a story, I demand an ending from destiny.

t_ch: 椅子斯基老师，既然你还活着，就请画完最后的对决吧！唯有如此，我才能甘心重新启程。
t_en: Miss Chairsky, since you're still alive, please finish drawing our final showdown! I need this if I am to fully accept the new journey ahead of me.

t_ch: 你想让我画出结局？可是…都已经过了这么久……
t_en: You want me to draw the ending? But... it's been so long...

t_ch: 其实，这也是椅子我的心愿呀。
t_en: Actually, I would like that too.

t_ch: 我一直觉得椅子斯基特别厉害，作为她的自画像，我每天都把椅背挺得直直的，眼睛睁得大大的！只有这样才配得上天才漫画家嘛！
t_en: I've always thought Chairsky to be really amazing. And since I'm her self-portrait, I make sure to keep my back straight and my eyes wide open every day! It's the only way I can do our genius comic artist justice.

t_ch: 那时候我还没见过你，但你的笔触很温柔，很幸福，我能感觉到，我正在被你爱着。
t_en: Even though I hadn't met you yet back then, your writing was so gentle and full of happiness, I already could feel your love for me.

t_ch: 所以我不会逼迫你去画这个结局…因为那么爱漫画的你，一定有你的不得已。
t_en: So I won't force you to draw this ending... because someone who loves comics as much as you do must have their reasons for not doing so.

t_ch: 她承受了很多漫画之外的压力。
t_en: She was under a lot of pressure from the world outside of the comic.

t_ch: 这就是你们说的「辛苦的普通人的现实生活」吗？
t_en: So this is what you call the "harsh realities of the ordinary people," huh?

t_ch: 老实讲，这也不普通了。有空你来后厨颠个勺，尝尝柴米油盐酱醋茶吧。
t_en: To be honest, this is far from ordinary. When you have the time, come to the kitchen and try your hand at cooking! I assure you that you'll get a good taste of day-to-day living then!

t_ch: 她无法承受画出结局的压力。
t_en: She couldn't handle the pressure of drawing the ending.

t_ch: 不想分别吗？那就用一个故事的结束，开启新的故事怎么样？
t_en: Don't want to say goodbye? Then how about using the ending of one story to start a new one?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，不是催更催开坑的意思。
t_en: Hehe, I'm not trying to pressure you into starting or updating anything.

t_ch: 其实十五年前…我就已经想出一个结局了。
t_en: Actually... I already came up with an ending <unbreak>15</unbreak> years ago.

t_ch: 真的吗！
t_en: Really!?

t_ch: 这是我在大纲中就设定好的结局，可是越往后画，我就越无法说服我自己：「这就是大家会喜欢的最好结局，这就是无数种可能性中最完美的结束。」
t_en: It was the ending I had in my outline. But as I drew, I couldn't convince myself that it was an ending everyone would love, and the perfect conclusion among countless possibilities.

t_ch: 不，只要是你，画下的就是最完美的！
t_en: Nah, anything you draw would be absolutely perfect!

t_ch: 别再这样吹捧我啦，椅子小姐。漫画家也是要吃饭的，我当然要考虑编辑的意见，考虑出版的看法，考虑读者的喜恶…可这样一考虑，完美就不存在了。
t_en: Don't put me on a pedestal like that, Miss Chair. Comic artists have to make a living too, you know. So of course, I had to take the editor's opinions, the publisher's views, and my readers' likes and dislikes into account... but once you start thinking about all that, perfection goes right out the window.

t_ch: 今天你不用考虑这些。
t_en: You don't have to worry about all that today.

t_ch: 完成比完美更重要。
t_en: Completion is more important than perfection.

t_ch: 你是在顾虑我吗？
t_en: Are you worried about me?

t_ch: 叫了我十五年阿梨，就忘了我是薇克丝了吗？对罪恶赶尽杀绝的私刑制裁者，早就有接受审判的觉悟了。
t_en: Have you actually forgotten that I'm Vexis just because you've been calling me Peary for <unbreak>15</unbreak> years? As a vigilante who punishes evil, I've long been ready to face judgment.

t_ch: 虽然也很不甘心，但如果对麦卡托来说，将我绳之以法，就是她哪怕拼尽所有也仍然值得的生活，那么她的心情，与追求普通生活的我也没什么两样嘛。
t_en: This may be hard for me to accept, but if bringing me to justice is what Mercator considers a life she's willing to give everything up for, then we're not so different after all. Because I feel the same way about living an ordinary life.

t_ch: 别犹豫了，漫画家椅子斯基。
t_en: So don't hesitate any longer, artist Chairsky.

t_ch: 还记得你在「作者寄语」里面写过无数的漂亮话吗？你说：「留在脑中的一流，比不上落在纸上的三流。」
t_en: Remember what you wrote in your "Notes From the Author"? You said, "A first-rate idea in your head is worth less than a third-rate one on paper."

t_ch: ——难道你其实并不真正相信这些话吗？
t_en: Don't tell me you don't actually believe in any of that?

t_ch: 别总想着压力、责任、一个人扛下一切之类的东西了。为这出结局精彩对弈直到分出胜负的，其实是漫画里的我和薇克丝啊！
t_en: Stop worrying about the pressure and responsibility. Remember, you don't have to shoulder it all by yourself. After all, the ones who played this thrilling game to its spectacular finale were actually Vexis and I in the comic!

t_ch: 就让这一切画上句号吧，看看她会否像个英雄那样生，我又会否如罪魁那般死。
t_en: Let's put an end to all of this. We'll see if she lives like a hero, and if I die like a villain.

t_ch: …我知道了。
t_en: ...Alright.

t_ch: 那时，麦卡托以M的名义寻求了治安局的合作，在她们最终对决的地方，设下了天罗地网。一旦薇克丝试图使用「麦卡托」的名字将她咒杀，就会立刻暴露，并遭到逮捕……
t_en: Back then, Mercator enlisted the Security Bureau's help in M's name, and set a trap at the site of their final confrontation. Had Vexis tried to use the name "Mercator" to kill her, she would have been immediately exposed and arrested.

t_ch: 自信从未使用过自己真名的麦卡托，抱着必胜的决心与曾经最好的搭档薇克丝相聚。
t_en: Mercator reunited with her former "best of partners," Vexis, determined to succeed and feeling extremely confident since she had never revealed her true name.

t_ch: 「米洛斯被杀案」中，遗落在现场的水笔是你惯用的品牌，他的心腹弗拉迪遭到咒杀后，我也在档案室监控里查到了酷似你的身影……
t_en: In the murder case of Milos, the pen left at the scene was your favorite brand, and after his confidant Frady was cursed to death, I also saw someone who looked remarkably like you in the archive room's surveillance footage.

t_ch: 而疑似犯罪高层的塔卡董事长，就死在你不得不将他无罪释放的第二天。
t_en: And Chairman Tacca, a suspected crime boss, died the very day after you were forced to release him without charges.

t_ch: 事到如今，你还不愿意承认你就是私刑制裁者吗？
t_en: Even now, you still won't admit you're the vigilante?

t_ch: 你调查的很仔细，「M」。
t_en: You've certainly done your homework, M.

t_ch: 你果然已经知道了我的身份。
t_en: So it's true. You already knew who I was.

t_ch: 我不仅知道你的身份，还知道一名高层刚刚被咒杀，现在所有埋伏着的治安官已经全都被调离去保护各位大人物了……
t_en: Not only do I know who you are, I also know that a high-ranking official has just been killed by a curse, and that all the security officers who were lying in wait have now been reassigned to VIP protection duty...

t_ch: 麦卡托，就像正义总被权力背叛一样，现在，你又一次被这个烂透的世界背叛了。
t_en: Mercator, just as justice is always betrayed by power, you have now, once again, been grossly let down by this world full of rot and filth.

t_ch: 麦卡托心如擂鼓，她明白自己已孤立无援，于是将手放入兜中，悄悄摁开了录音。
t_en: Mercator's heart was pounding like a drum. Realizing she was completely on her own now, she slipped her hand into her pocket and quietly pressed the Record button.

t_ch: 所以…就必须要杀人吗？
t_en: So... do you really have to kill someone?

t_ch: 既然世界有太多的腐朽，那么就用死亡剔骨剜肉…我们曾是最好的搭档，你一定能理解我，也愿意帮助我的，对吧？
t_en: Since there's so much rot and filth in this world, we should excise it all with death... We were once the best of partners. Surely you understand me, and you'll be willing to help me, right?

t_ch: ——继续做我的搭档，与我携手，去创造那个绝对正义的新世界吧！
t_en: So be my best partner again, and together, we will create a new world of absolute justice!

t_ch: 不，无论出于什么样的理由，建立在杀人之上的正义不可能是真正的正义！收手吧，薇克丝，就算今天只剩我一个人，我也要逮捕你。
t_en: No. Whatever the reason, justice built on murder can never be true justice. Give it up, Vexis. Even if I'm the only one left, I will arrest you no matter what.

t_ch: 那么，只能和你说再见了，「麦克唐娜」。
t_en: Well. I guess this means goodbye, then... McDonna.

t_ch: 你…怎么会知道我……
t_en: H—how... d—did you know...

t_ch: 知道「麦卡托」根本不是你的真名？因为我其实根本不想杀人啊！咒杀「拉德克」的那一天，我真的太害怕了，于是我对自己下咒…咒杀的内容是——
t_en: How did I know that "Mercator" isn't your true name, you ask? Because I never wanted to kill anyone! The day I cursed Radek to death, I was so terrified, I put a curse on myself...

t_ch: 「双手沾染鲜血的薇克丝，被正义的麦卡托杀死！」
t_en: "Vexis, whose hands were covered in blood, was slain by the righteous Mercator!"

t_ch: 我平静地等待死亡，然而，什么也没有发生。
t_en: Then, I just waited for death to take me, but nothing happened.

t_ch: 此时我终于理解了——我最好的搭档「麦卡托」从未对我袒露真名。
t_en: That's when it finally dawned on me: Mercator, my "best of partners," had never revealed her true name to me.

t_ch: 你看见我去档案室查监控的那一天，我不仅查到了「弗拉迪」的名字，也查到了你的名字，「麦克唐娜」。
t_en: The day you saw me checking the surveillance footage from the archive room, I uncovered the name "Frady" as well as yours, "McDonna."

t_ch: 可我从来…从来没有想过要咒杀你，因为我们曾经是最好的搭档，就连随身带录音笔的习惯，也是我教给你的。可惜了，也是因为这个习惯，今天我必须和你说再见了。
t_en: But I never... never wanted to curse you to death, because we used to be the best partners, and I was the one who taught you to always carry a voice recorder on you. And unfortunately, it's that very habit that forces me to bid you farewell today.

t_ch: ——如果我死了的话，这个世界将再次被腐朽的人充斥。所以安眠吧，麦克唐娜。
t_en: If I die, this world will once again be filled with people tainted by rot and filth. So rest in peace, McDonna.

t_ch: 也就是说，我在许多年前就输了吗？
t_en: Are you saying that I had already failed years ago?

t_ch: 不，你没有输，麦卡托，我不会让你输。
t_en: No, you haven't failed, Mercator. I would never let you fail.

t_ch: 我会带着你的愿望，去创造那个绝对正义的新世界。在那里，纯白的{RUBY_B#麦卡托}「正义」{RUBY_E#}必胜。
t_en: I will carry your wishes with me and build a new, absolutely just world. There, pure {RUBY_B#Mercator}"justice"{RUBY_E#} shall prevail.

t_ch: ……哈哈，什么嘛。
t_en: ...Hahaha! What's with that.

t_ch: 这不是个很好的结局吗！怎么十五年前不画出来呢？
t_en: This is a pretty good ending! Why didn't you draw it <unbreak>15</unbreak> years ago?

t_ch: 麦卡托大笑着，消失在了你的眼前。
t_en: Mercator laughs and vanishes before your eyes.

t_ch: 结束了。
t_en: It's over.

t_ch: 原来…是一个这样的结局吗？
t_en: So... this is how it ends, huh?

t_ch: 十五年前的审美里，这种反英雄人物就很受欢迎嘛。那时候正懦弱又幼稚的我，就喜欢薇克丝这样杀伐果断的人。
t_en: <unbreak>15</unbreak> years ago, anti-hero characters like these were really popular. At the time, when I was still weak and immature, I found myself drawn to decisive and ruthless characters like Vexis.

t_ch: 麦卡托会去哪里？
t_en: Where will Mercator go?

t_ch: 她真的死了吗？
t_en: Is she really dead?

t_ch: 只是执念消散了吧。
t_en: It's just the obsession that's faded away, that's all.

t_ch: 真羡慕她啊，可以为了一件事彻底燃烧……
t_en: I really admire her, being able to burn with such complete passion for one purpose...

t_ch: 她也很羡慕你吧？羡慕你们真的可以放下宏大的执念，只活在当下，体认生活。
t_en: She must really admire you too, given how you're able to let go of those grandiose obsessions and simply live fully in the present.

t_ch: 别弄得像追悼会一样啊！
t_en: Stop making this sound like a memorial service!

t_ch: 啊抱歉抱歉，感觉气氛到了嘛。
t_en: Ah, my apologies! I just got caught up in the moment.

t_ch: 我也羡慕她。
t_en: I admire her too.

t_ch: 羡慕她什么呢？
t_en: What do you admire her for?

t_ch: 羡慕她坦言要做一个英雄。
t_en: I admire how she openly states her desire to be a hero.

t_ch: 承认不被人理解的理想，确实很勇敢。
t_en: It takes real courage to stand by your dream when no one else understands it.

t_ch: 羡慕她像个英雄那样死去。
t_en: I admire how she died like a hero.

t_ch: 她如愿追求了她想要的超凡。
t_en: She pursued the extraordinary life, and got what she wanted.

t_ch: 羡慕她很快就领悟了「开拓」。
t_en: I admire how quickly she grasped the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 你看起来倒是很欣慰的样子。
t_en: You do seem rather gratified.

t_ch: 我不知道，羡慕就对了。
t_en: I don't know what to say. I just know that I do admire her.

t_ch: 好了各位，我该回家给孩子辅导作业了。乐子神在上，希望我能保持好脸色。
t_en: Alright folks, I have to head home and help my kid with his homework. By The Laughter, I hope I can keep my cool.

t_ch: 至于麦卡托在漫画中的未来——
t_en: As for Mercator's fate in the comic...

t_ch: 就交给你了。
t_en: It's all in your hands now.

t_ch: 过你的小日子去吧！
t_en: Go live out your ordinary days!

t_ch: 也谢谢你的帮助，没有你，我可能到现在也依旧在逃避。
t_en: And thank you for your help. Without you, I might still be running away from everything.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 再见，朋友。
t_en: See you, my friend.

t_ch: 你会重新画漫画吗？
t_en: Will you go back to drawing comic now?

t_ch: 也许吧…也许。
t_en: Maybe... just maybe.

t_ch: 那我…那我……
t_en: Then I... I...

t_ch: 你跟我走，漫画家总会需要一张跑很快的椅子。
t_en: Come with me. A comic artist always needs a chair that can zoom around fast.

t_ch: 啊？到底是什么使用场景啦！
t_en: Huh? What's with that weird scenario of being used?

t_ch: 一段时间后……
t_en: Some time later...

t_ch: 说时迟那时快！椅子斯基面对二十多家媒体，十多家出版商的围追堵截，坐上一把会喷气的椅子撒腿就跑，除了一只狸猫，谁也没能追上她！
t_en: It all happened in a flash! Chairsky, cornered by over <unbreak>20</unbreak> media outlets and a dozen publishers, jumped onto her jet-powered chair and made a break for it! And nobody could catch up to her... except for one furbo!

t_ch: 于是那把椅子就替她接受了狸猫的独家专访！
t_en: And so, that very chair ended up taking her place for the furbo's exclusive interview!

t_ch: 这都什么跟什么？
t_en: What in the world is going on?

t_ch: 这是《复活吧！椅子斯基！》的续作，纪实类读本《还真*挺萌的*复活了！椅子斯基！》。
t_en: This is a documentary-style account titled D*mn, Chairsky Has Really Risen Again!, the sequel to Rise Again, Chairsky!.

t_ch: 对对对，详情请见狸狸通信！
t_en: That's right, that's right! Check out Furbobocom for all the details!

t_ch: 打广告出门右转，不送！
t_en: Take your advertising somewhere else, buddy! And don't let the door hit ya on the way out!

t_ch: 漫画家真的需要跑很快的椅子！
t_en: Comic artists do have a need fast-rolling chairs!

t_ch: 可不是吗？这使用场景谁想得到？
t_en: Right? Who would've predicted that it'll be used like that?

t_ch: 你怎么改行说书了。
t_en: Since when did you become a storyteller?

t_ch: 兼职！兼职！
t_en: It's just a part-time gig!

t_ch: 想买点传奇复活作者椅子斯基的新作吗？《麦卡托事件簿》正在火热销售中！
t_en: Want to check out the new work by Chairsky, the artist who famously came back to life? The Mercator Files is now flying off the shelves at a store near you!

t_ch: 真没想到，椅子斯基老师最后还是回来画漫画了。更没想到，我还会以这样的方式和麦卡托重逢。
t_en: I never expected Miss Chairsky to go back to drawing comic again. And I definitely didn't expect her to reunite with Mercator in this way.

t_ch: 薇克丝！
t_en: Vexis!

t_ch: 月梨！
t_en: Pearuna!

t_ch: 好久不见了，朋友。我已经成功在异常防御部拿到了合法身份，现在，你可以称呼我的本名——薇克丝了。
t_en: Long time no see, my friend. I've officially received legal recognition from the Department of Aberration Defense, so from now on, you can call me by my real name: Vexis.

t_ch: 事实证明，椅子斯基老师可能是个很好的厨师，但并不是个很好的老板。「爆炒堂」终于倒闭了，她也「不得不」重操旧业——依我看，这无非是她给自己找个台阶下罢了。
t_en: As it turns out, while Miss Chairsky might be a great cook, she isn't so great at running a business. Wok 'n' Roll eventually went under, and she "had to" return to her old profession. But if you ask me, she was simply looking for an excuse.

t_ch: 以前，椅子斯基老师因为无法承受「知名漫画家」所要承受的压力，选择了假死。而这一次，她终于不必独自承受那些压力了。
t_en: Back then, Miss Chairsky chose to feign her death because she couldn't handle the pressure that came with being a famous comic artist. But this time, she doesn't have to shoulder all that pressure by herself.

t_ch: 你看这本《麦卡托事件簿》的封面——「原作：椅子小姐；作画：椅子斯基」。
t_en: Look at the cover of The Mercator Files. It says "Story by Miss Chair, Art by Chairsky."

t_ch: 啧啧，把最吸引火力的身份交给椅子小姐，自己退居幕后，安心画画…真狡猾啊，椅子斯基老师。
t_en: Tsk, having Miss Chair take all the heat while you sit back and quietly draw behind the scenes... how sly of you, Miss Chairsky.

t_ch: 希望这次她不会逃跑了！
t_en: I hope she won't run away again this time!

t_ch: 不会的。那把椅子飞得那么快，她逃不脱的。
t_en: She won't. That chair zips around so quickly, there's no way she will be able to shake it off her tail, even if she tried.

t_ch: 椅子小姐不会承受不住压力吧？
t_en: Would Miss Chair be able to take the pressure though?

t_ch: 有可能…但她是一把椅子诶？「承受压力」不应该是她最擅长的事吗？
t_en: I'm not sure... but she's a chair, isn't she? Isn't "handling pressure" what she's designed to do?

t_ch: 但愿这一次，无论是追求超凡的人，还是追求平凡的人，都能得偿所愿。
t_en: Hopefully this time, whether they're chasing the extraordinary or embracing the ordinary, everyone gets what they wish for.

t_ch: 你继续逛吧，我该给我家那臭小子买补习册了。有缘再见。
t_en: Have fun hanging around here. I have to go buy some workbooks for my little rascal at home. See you around!

t_ch: 薇克丝小姐！没想到会在这里遇到你。方便的话…能不能给我签个名？
t_en: Miss Vexis! What a surprise to see you here. If it's not too much trouble... could I get your autograph?

t_ch: 当然可以。不过…我记得你不是麦卡托的过激粉丝吗？怎么来找我签名了？
t_en: Of course. But... I seem to recall that you were a hardcore Mercator fan? So why ask me for my autograph?

t_ch: 嗐…我一时半刻是见不到麦卡托了，这不就只好退而求其次……
t_en: *Sigh*... Since I won't be seeing Mercator anytime soon, I guess I'll just have to settle for second best...

t_ch: 信不信我咒杀你哦？
t_en: You wanna get cursed to death right here and now?

t_ch: 你们不要再打了啦！
t_en: Stop fighting, please!

t_ch: 那你就别拱火了！
t_en: Then quit fanning the flames!

t_ch: 不过，我刚刚倒是发现了一个有趣的东西，就当送给你这个麦卡托粉的见面礼吧。
t_en: But hey, I just found something interesting, so consider it a little meet-and-greet gift for a fellow Mercator fan.

t_ch: 不死途和你接过一本崭新的《麦卡托事件簿》第一册，在它的扉页上，不知是谁龙飞凤舞地写了一行字：
t_en: What Ashveil and you take is the first volume of The Mercator Files, and on the frontispiece, someone has left a line in bold, flowing writing:

t_ch: 「这是我最后的委托：看住她们两个，别再断更了。——麦卡托」
t_en: "This is my final assignment for you: Keep an eye on those two, and don't let them skip out on more updates. — Mercator"

t_ch: 谢谢，呜呜，谢谢！
t_en: Thank you. *sniffles* Thank you!

t_ch: 薇克丝必胜！
t_en: Victory to Vexis!

t_ch: 真是不可理喻…
t_en: How unreasonable...

t_ch: （似乎有人在吵架？别打起来吧…要不去凑个热…拉个架！过去看看吧。）
t_en: (Sounds like someone's arguing? Hope it doesn't turn into a fight... Maybe I should go check it out— I mean break it up! Let's take a look.)

t_ch: （要帮雪衣买些什么同人志呢？去旁边那个摊位上看看吧……）
t_en: (What kind of fan comic should I get for Xueyi today? Let's check out that booth over there...)

t_ch: 不愧是来自大热门漫画的「球棒浣熊」！
t_en: As expected of the "Baseball Raccoon" from that super popular comic!

t_ch: 我真不是漫画角色……
t_en: Seriously, I'm not a comic character...

t_ch: 「爆炒堂」…就是这里了！门口似乎有位店员，去找她问问吧。
t_en: "Wok 'n' Roll"... This must be the place! Looks like there's a clerk at the door, let's go ask her.

t_ch: 椅子小姐呢？
t_en: Where's Miss Chair?

t_ch: 你跑得太慢啦！
t_en: Come on, hurry up!

t_ch: 快，薇克丝就在前面！
t_en: Quick, I see Vexis right over there!

t_ch: 我先去拦住她！
t_en: I'll go and stop her!

t_ch: 哦，瞧瞧，她一直跟在你们身后。
t_en: Oh, look who's been following you guys around.

t_ch: 试试这里的「二次元JUMP！」跃迁吧！
t_en: Try out the "Interplanar Jump!" warp here!

t_ch: 你成功追上椅子，拦住了「月梨」。
t_en: You successfully catch up to Chair and stop "Pearuna."

t_ch: 竟然还能这么走？你脑子真好使啊！
t_en: Wait, you can actually do that? You're a genius!

t_ch: 普通型号的机铠，在二维市的街道上随处可见…如果它没有被随意停放在路边的话。
t_en: Normal mechatrons are a common sight cruising the streets of Duomension City... if they aren't just randomly parked on the sidewalks.

t_ch: （敲敲它的胸口）
t_en: (Knocks on its chest)

t_ch: 或许是随身携带球棒的缘故，你忽地感觉手痒难耐、渴望破坏，而这台机铠…以它的结实程度，轻轻敲几下应该不成问题——
t_en: Maybe it's the bat you're carrying, but your fingers suddenly itch with the irresistible urge to break something. And this mechatron... it's sturdy enough that a few taps shouldn't hurt.

t_ch: 别、别敲了！震得我胸口疼，哎呦。
t_en: S—Stop banging! The vibrations are killing my chest, ow!

t_ch: （无视它）
t_en: (Ignore it)

t_ch: 是啊。在这二相乐园，机铠多得就像是路边的尘灵，区区破铜烂铁，自然不会引起你的兴趣。正当你打算离开时——
t_en: Yeah. Here in Planarcadia, mechatrons are as common as wispae on the street. Such scrap metals aren't worth your attention, just as you're about to leave...

t_ch: …可恶。竟敢无视我！
t_en: ...Darn it. How dare you ignore me!
